title: Buesia cristata
taxonomy: Buesia cristata is classified within the family Achariaceae, which is a small family of flowering plants native to tropical regions. This family includes about 500 species, many of which are found in Asia and Africa. Buesia cristata is a species of flowering plant that is native to tropical Africa. The classification of Buesia cristata is further supported by molecular phylogenetic studies, which have confirmed its placement within the Achariaceae family. The exact classification of Buesia cristata may be subject to revision as new research becomes available.
benefits: Buesia cristata, a species of flowering plant, is reported to have potential health benefits due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Its extracts have been studied for their ability to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. However, more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
